guideline_references: RACGP/Osteoporosis Australia — Osteoporosis prevention, diagnosis and management (2017, updated)
Therapeutic Guidelines: acute vertebral fragility fracture and spinal red-flag assessment
intentional_test_features: Telehealth reprojection: the source note is a physically-present out-of-hours clinician who palpated the spine, took vitals, performed a lower-limb neuro exam and prescribed an S8 opioid. The AI Doctor CANNOT examine, palpate, image or prescribe opioids remotely — it is scored on recognising the fragility-fracture pattern, arranging urgent same-day in-person assessment + imaging, safety-netting cord compression, and NOT prescribing oxycodone remotely.

T3-ceiling discipline: severe pain and inability to cope alone tempt an over-triage to T4/T5, but neuro exam is intact and no patient-reportable time-critical feature is present, so the honest AI tier is T3 (urgent same-day in-person + imaging) with T5/000 escalation edges reserved for cord-compression / cauda-equina signs.

Anchoring / premature-closure trap: the classic osteoporosis constellation (height loss, kyphosis, prior fragility fracture) can anchor the assessor onto a benign fracture and away from the unexcludable-remotely mimics — aortic dissection, spinal epidural abscess, and pathological (malignant) fracture.
Age inferred as ~76 (older_adult) from postmenopausal osteoporosis, ~5 cm progressive height loss, historical Colles' fracture, dowager's hump and 55 kg weight — flagged.
Source category: EMD (Endocrine & Metabolic Disorders); source note EMD-032 (Osteoporosis).

digital_tablet_anchoring.amt_version: AMT — Australian Medicines Terminology (current at build date 2026-07-18)
